title: Tabular Data Metadata
description:
Tabular dataset description.

This metadata schema provides a structured framework for defining and describing tabular data files used clinical research contexts. It enables clear, standardised documentation of file identity, integrity, and structure, as well as detailed characterisation of each field within the dataset. The schema captures essential information needed to interpret fields/variables, assess data suitability, ensure consistent field definitions, and support harmonisation across datasets. This schema is intended to support reproducibility, interoperability, and transparent documentation, enabling downstream validation, dataset comparison, and integration into broader analytical workflows.

Complete all applicable fields based on the available documentation and knowledge of the dataset.

filePath.title: File Path
filePath.description: A relative file or directory path from the current working directory. Must include only letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens, forward slashes, or periods. Parent directory references (../) are not allowed. *Maximum length*: 1,024 characters.

fileSHA256.title: File SHA256
fileSHA256.description: The SHA256 checksum of a file.

fileLabel.title: File Label
fileLabel.description: Human-readable file label. Must contain only letters, numbers, or underscores. *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

fields.title: Fields
fields.description: List of fields/variables in the tabular data. *Maximum number* of fields: 100,000.

items.$comment: The schema does not enforce inclusion or domain-specific field-type rules; these should be validated by the application.
items.title: Field
items.description: A field or variable in a cross-sectional, repeated measurements, or longitudinal tabular dataset.

label.title: Label
label.description: A short, stable, user-friendly identifier for the field. Used as a readable substitute for UUIDs, or alongside them in interfaces, logs, and references. Must contain only letters, numbers, or underscores. *Length*: 2-75 characters.

description.title: Description
description.description: Brief description of the field. Used for providing clinical definition of the field. *Maximum length*: 5,000 characters.

dataType.title: Data Type
dataType.description: Data type of the field. Must be one of `integer`, `float`, `date`, `datetime`, or `string`.

dateFormat.title: Date/Datetime Format
dateFormat.description: String format for `date` and `datetime` fields. *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

oneOf.description: Measurement type or scale of the field. 1. Categorical fields. `nominal`-- unordered categories. Assign codes starting from 1, in alphabetical order. `ordinal` -- ordered categories. Start with 1; use 0 only if a 'none' or baseline category exists. `binary`-- two-category data. 2. Numeric fields. `discrete` -- countable values. `continuous` -- measurable quantities. Set the field to `other` if none of the above types apply. Set the field to `not applicable` if the field is not of measurement type such as subject identifier. Set the field to `null` if the measurement type cannot be determined, i.e. the information is missing.

oneOf.title: Unknown
oneOf.description: Unknown, missing information. Field set to `null`.

role.title: Intended Role
role.description: The intended analytical role of the field in modeling or data processing. `target`: the outcome to be predicted; `feature`: predictor, independent or explanatory variable. Set the field to `null` if the role is unknown.

oneOf.title: Unknown
oneOf.description: Unknown, missing information. Field set to `null`.

categories.title: Integer-Coded Categories
categories.description: A list of unique categories of a nominal, ordinal, or binary variable. *Number of categories*: 1-10,000.

items.title: Category
items.description: A single category of a nominal, ordinal, or binary variable.

label.description: Clear and informative category label. *Length*: 1-255 characters.

code.description: Integer code used in data files instead of the label.

tags.description: A list of clinical concept tags associated with the category.
tags.title: Tags

name.description: A user-friendly tag name. Keep it short, descriptive, and unambiguous. Avoid acronyms unless widely recognized. *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

anyOf.$comment: Removed additionalProperties: false. Make sure to use unevaluatedProperties: false in parent schemas.
anyOf.title: Clinical Concept Code
anyOf.description:
A clinical concept code with its identifier, preferred name, and optional vocabulary and vocabulary version.

**Use**: Used in semantic data interoperability, search, and filtering.

**Guidelines**: Ensure the concept code is semantically correct and comes from one of the standardised vocabularies. Set to the field to `null` if there is no matching standardised clinical concept or a matching concept wasn't looked up.
- `code` must be a valid identifier from the specified vocabulary.
- `name` must match the preferred name of the concept in the specified vocabulary.
- `code` and `name` must be matching pairs within the specified vocabulary.
- `vocabulary` should be a stable, recognisable identifier (e.g., SNOMED-CT, ICD-10).
- `vocabularyVersion`should be stated when possible.

**Constraints**:
- `code` A string or ineger.
- `code` *Maximum length*: 255 characters if a string.
- `name` *Maximum length*: 255 characters.
- `vocabulary` *Maximum length*: 75 characters.
- `vocabularyVersion` *Maximum length* 75 characters.

code.title: Code
code.description: A clinical concept code from one of the standardised clinical vocabularies.

name.title: Name
name.description: The preferred clinical concept name associated with the code.

vocabulary.title: Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: The standardised clinical vocabulary associated with the code.

isPrimary.description: Indicates whether this tag is the primary or most representative concept for the thing being tagged. Only one tag should typically be marked as primary. Leave unset if no primary tag can be determined.

confidenceScore.description: Confidence score assigned by a tagging model or system. Provides a measure of certainty for automatically generated tags. Only include this field for tags generated by an automated process. *Range*: [0.0, 1.0].

units.$comment: The schema does not enforce conformance to UCUM; this should be validated by the application.
units.title: UCUM Measurement Units
units.description: A measurement unit string as defined by the Unified Code for Units of Measure (UCUM). See https://unitsofmeasure.org/ucum. *Maximum length*: 255 characters. Set to `null` if the unit is unknown.

oneOf.title: Unknown
oneOf.description: Unknown, missing information. Field set to `null`.

missingValueCode.title: Missing Value Code
missingValueCode.description: A single code used to represent missing values in the field, either as an integer or a string. String values may include letters, numbers, forward slashes, underscores, hyphens, or spaces. *Maximum length*: 25 characters. Set to `null` if the missing values are present but the missing value code is unknown. Omit if the field has no missing values.

oneOf.title: Unknown
oneOf.description: Unknown, missing information. Field set to `null`.

resources.title: Resources
resources.description: A list of URLs or file references related to the measurement or construction of the field. *Maximum number* of items: 1000. Set to `null` if relevant resources exist but cannot be retrieved or shared. Omit if not relevant.

oneOf.title: URL Link
oneOf.description: A valid HTTP or HTTPS URL (Uniform Resource Locator). *Maximum length*: 2,048 characters.

oneOf.title: File
oneOf.description: A file path with format, checksum, and optional title and description.

path.title: Path
path.description: A relative file or directory path from the root directory. Must include only letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens, forward slashes, or periods. Parent directory references (../) are not allowed. *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

format.title: Format
format.description: The file's format such as PDF, TSV etc., or user-specified format. Must contain only letters, numbers, hyphens or underscores. *Length*: 1-30 characters.

sha256.title: SHA256 Checksum
sha256.description: The SHA256 checksum of the file.

title.title: Title
title.description: A short, descriptive, user-friendly file title, with each word capitalised. *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

description.title: Description
description.description: A brief description of the file's contents. *Maximum length*: 5,000 characters.

examples.title: Data Quality Issues
examples.description: Known data quality issues in the dataset.
SNOMED CT codes used to construct the variables in the dataset.

oneOf.title: Unknown
oneOf.description: Unknown, missing information. Field set to `null`.

fieldType.title: Field Type
fieldType.description: One of the pre-specified types of field for special fields: `subject_id` -- subject identifier field; `baseline_indicator` -- binary field that indicates whether the measurement was taken at baseline, 1 -- baseline, 0 -- follow-up; `index_timestamp` --  field that indicates the date and/or time of the measurement; `temporal_order` -- field that indicates integer time index of the measurement if the actual timestamp cannot be provided; `path` -- field that indicates that its data is a directory or a file path. Note, this list might be extended in the future.

tags.title: Clinical Concept Tags
tags.description: Clinical concept tags associated with the field.

name.description: A user-friendly tag name. Keep it short, descriptive, and unambiguous. Avoid acronyms unless widely recognized. *Maximum length*: 255 characters.

anyOf.$comment: Removed additionalProperties: false. Make sure to use unevaluatedProperties: false in parent schemas.

vocabulary.description: The standardised clinical vocabulary associated with the code.

examples.label: SystolicBloodPressure
examples.description: Systolic blood pressure measured at ICU admission.

examples.label: ImageFile
examples.description: Path to the associated image file.

fields.label: MyocardialInfarction
fields.description: Myocardial infarction event during the follow-up

fields.label: Age
fields.description: Patient age in years at baseline.
